1: # ox_data — Data Services Module
2: 
3: The data services module provides a generic, extensible data object model. At its core is
4: `GenericDataObject` — a pure data container responsible only for storing typed attributes,
5: converting between types, and firing callbacks on mutations. Everything else (persistence,
6: transactions, validation, cross-datasource queries, remote access) is an addon system that
7: builds on top of the core without modifying it.
8: 
9: ---
10: 
11: ## Architecture
12: 
13: ```
14: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
15: │                       Application Code                          │
16: └──┬─────────────┬──────────────┬───────────────┬────────────────┘
17:    │             │              │               │
18:    ▼             ▼              ▼               ▼
19: Persistent   Transactable  Validatable   DataObjectManager
20: (addon)      (addon)        (addon)        (addon)
21:    │             │              │               │
22:    └─────────────┴──────────────┴───────────────┘
23:                          │ all addons operate on
24:                          ▼
25: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
26: │                  GenericDataObject                              │
27: │  attributes: HashMap<String, AttributeValue>  ← typed data     │
28: │  extensions: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> ← addon state  │
29: │  callbacks:  CallbackManager  ← per-object hooks               │
30: │  identifier_name: String                                        │
31: └──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
32:                            │
33:           ┌────────────────┴────────────────┐
34:           ▼                                 ▼
35: ┌─────────────────────┐         ┌────────────────────────┐
36: │  ox_type_converter  │         │     ox_persistence     │
37: │  ValueType          │         │  PersistenceDriver tr. │
38: │  ConversionRegistry │         │  PERSISTENCE_DRIVER_   │
39: │  TypeConverter      │         │  REGISTRY              │
40: └─────────────────────┘         └──────────┬─────────────┘
41:                                            │
42:                    ┌───────────────────────┼──────────────────────┐
43:                    ▼                       ▼                      ▼
44:         ┌──────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐
45:         │  File/JSON/YAML  │  │  ox_persistence_gdo_   │  │  SQL drivers │
46:         │  drivers         │  │  relational (wrapper)  │  │  pg/sqlite/… │
47:         └──────────────────┘  └────────────────────────┘  └──────────────┘
48: 
49: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
50: │                  ox_data_object_manager                         │
51: │  DataDictionary   DataObjectManager   QueryEngine               │
52: │  cross-datasource joins, attribute remapping, cardinality       │
53: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
54: 
55: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
56: │  ox_workflow Plugins (cdylib)                                   │
57: │  ox_data_broker                — REST API for data ops         │
58: │  ox_data_object_dictionary_manager — dictionary CRUD REST API  │
59: │  ox_persistence_datasource_manager — datasource CRUD           │
60: │  ox_persistence_driver_manager     — driver lifecycle          │
61: │  ox_persistence_driver_installer   — package installation      │
62: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
63: ```
64: 
65: ---
66: 
67: ## Design Principles
68: 
69: **GDO is data only.** `GenericDataObject` stores named, typed values. It knows nothing
70: about persistence, locking, workflows, or any domain concept. Addon systems receive a GDO,
71: operate on it, and store their own state in its extension slot — the GDO carries that data
72: but never interprets it.
73: 
74: **Addons, not wrappers.** Persistence, transactions, validation, and other capabilities
75: are separate systems that work with `GenericDataObject` instances. They use the object's
76: extension slot to attach their own state so no combination of capabilities requires a
77: purpose-built wrapper struct.
78: 
79: **Universal callbacks.** Every data manipulation — in the GDO core and in every addon —
80: fires `before_*` / `after_*` / `on_error_*` events through the GDO's two-level callback
81: dispatch: per-object callbacks first, then the global `CALLBACK_MANAGER`.
82: 
83: **Extensions are serialized.** The extension slot round-trips through
84: `to_serializable_map()` / `from_serializable_map()` as opaque JSON. Addon state (such as
85: which driver an object came from, or its lock status) survives serialization.
86: 
87: **Transactions wrap locking.** When a transaction is active on a GDO, multi-container
88: saves are atomic: all succeed or all are rolled back. Without an active transaction,
89: failures are logged and execution continues.
90: 
91: **Drivers are FFI plugins.** Persistence drivers are dynamically loaded shared libraries
92: (`.so`/`.dylib`/`.dll`) that export a standard C ABI.
93: 
94: **Type safety across boundaries.** `ox_type_converter` provides a central registry of
95: conversions between any two `ValueType`s. `GenericDataObject::get<T>` automatically
96: converts stored values to the requested Rust type.
97: 
98: ---
99: 
100: ## Component Specs
101: 
102: | Spec | Contents |
103: |------|----------|
104: | [spec/core.md](spec/core.md) | `GenericDataObject`, `AttributeValue`, callbacks, extension slot, `Introspectable` trait |
105: | [spec/types.md](spec/types.md) | `ValueType`, `TypeConverter`, `ConversionRegistry` |
106: | [spec/persistence.md](spec/persistence.md) | `PersistenceDriver`, `Persistent`, `DataObjectState`, `discard` |
107: | [spec/transactions.md](spec/transactions.md) | `Transactable`, `LockStatus`, force unlock, TTL, transaction semantics |
108: | [spec/validation.md](spec/validation.md) | `ValidationRule`, built-in rules, `ValidationSet`, `Validatable` |
109: | [spec/dictionary.md](spec/dictionary.md) | `DataDictionary`, `DataObjectManager`, cardinality, relationships |
110: | [spec/query.md](spec/query.md) | `QueryEngine`, `QueryPlan`, `QueryNode`, join execution, filters |
111: | [spec/introspection.md](spec/introspection.md) | `ox_introspection`, `ObjectSchema`, `FieldDescriptor`, forms integration |
112: | [spec/drivers.md](spec/drivers.md) | Driver FFI ABI (incl. `ox_driver_delete`), management plugins |

114: | [spec/broker.md](spec/broker.md) | `ox_data_broker` REST API, pagination, delete endpoint |

117: 
118: ## Crate Map
119: 
120: | Crate | Type | Depends On |
121: |-------|------|-----------|
122: | `ox_type_converter` | lib | — |
123: | `ox_callback_manager` | lib | — |
124: | `ox_data_object` | lib | `ox_type_converter`, `ox_callback_manager` |
125: | `ox_persistence` | lib | `ox_data_object`, `ox_type_converter` |
126: | `ox_transaction` | lib | `ox_data_object`, `ox_persistence`, `ox_callback_manager` |
127: | `ox_validation` | lib | `ox_data_object`, `ox_callback_manager` |
128: | `ox_data_object_manager` | lib | `ox_data_object`, `ox_persistence`, `ox_type_converter` |
129: | `ox_introspection` | lib | `ox_data_object`, `ox_data_object_manager`, `ox_validation` |
130: | `ox_data_object_dictionary_manager` | cdylib plugin | `ox_data_object_manager`, `ox_workflow_abi` |
131: | `ox_persistence_gdo_relational` | cdylib driver | `ox_persistence`, `ox_data_object`, `ox_type_converter` |
132: | `ox_persistence_driver_manager` | cdylib plugin | `ox_persistence`, `ox_workflow_abi` |
133: | `ox_persistence_driver_installer` | cdylib plugin | `ox_persistence`, `ox_fileproc`, `ox_workflow_abi` |
134: | `ox_persistence_datasource_manager` | cdylib plugin | `ox_persistence`, `ox_data_object_manager`, `ox_workflow_abi`, `ox_forms_api` |
135: | `ox_data_broker` | cdylib plugin | `ox_persistence`, `ox_transaction`, `ox_callback_manager`, `ox_workflow_abi`, `ox_fileproc` |
136: 
137: Sample Workflow:
138: Install and enable driver
139: configure datasource
140: introspect datasource to get field definition
141: store in data dictionary
142: -- rich field definitions to help users understand the data
143: -- relationships between data within each datastore and between datastores
144: 	--- should support multiple join strategies
145: -assemble data objects from field definitions
146: -- stored in data dictionary
147: -- primary datastore/datastore container for data object as base for hydrating data
148: -- data objects can contain fields from multiple datastores
149: -rich description of dataobject and usage
150: -define data rules
151: instantiate instance of dataobject by definition name
152: set values of field to use for filtering list or hydrating single object
153: get paginated list or hydrate single object
154: work with data
155: - Form built from meta information or manual
156: -- Bind attributes to form fields
157: -- Client side data rules built from meta information
158: -- Multiuser change monitoring and real time updates using websockets
159: -- submit changes
160: - apply data rules server side
